items.title: England experiences more Argentina agony at World Cup, but it won't stop them dreaming
items.summary: England aren't going to be bringing the World Cup home, 60 years of hurt due to extend at least another four years after the devastation of another semifinal defeat.

items.title: World Cup final: The story behind Lionel Messi's famous photo with a baby Lamine Yamal
items.summary: On Monday morning, Argentinian legend Lionel Messi will go up against rising Spanish superstar Lamine Yamal in the World Cup final, 19 years after their famous photo shoot.

items.title: League bans Henty footballer Grady Nigsch for 'life', advises opponent to go to police
items.summary: A player and assistant coach for a southern NSW AFL team has been handed a "life ban" following a tribunal hearing into eight offences.

items.title: UK's Reform proposes round-the-clock protection for MPs after politician's death
items.summary: Britain's populist Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, has called for all lawmakers to be given "full security" if they want it after the alleged murder of Ann Widdecombe, a prominent member of the party.

items.title: Argentina players hold up 'Falklands are Argentine' banner after World Cup victory over England
items.summary: Argentinian stars hold up a 'The Falklands are Argentine' banner after defeating England at the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: Thomas Tuchel has 'no regrets' as tactics questioned in World Cup capitulation
items.summary: Thomas Tuchel defends his tactics after his team became just the second in World Cup history to lose a semifinal after scoring the first goal.

items.title: World Cup quick hits: England boss Thomas Tuchel's tactics allow Lionel Messi to orchestrate Argentina win
items.summary: England's World Cup ends in heartbreak again, with questions being asked of its manager's decisions as Lionel Messi plotted Argentina's win and the Three Lions' demise. Here are the quick hits.

items.title: Lionel Messi's World Cup magic inspires Argentina as history beckons in final against Spain
items.summary: With England leading 1-0 and Argentina's hopes of back-to-back World Cup titles fading, superstar Lionel Messi once again becomes the hero.

items.title: Ancestral remains of Rapa Nui people begin long journey home after 143 years at Australian Museum
items.summary: The remains of members of the Rapa Nui people from Easter Island are "finally going home" after sitting in the Australian Museum's collection for over a century.

items.title: Six months after the alleged Invasion Day terror attack, inquiry hears stories of fear
items.summary: A day of evidence reflecting on racism, hate and violence directed towards Aboriginal people highlights the ongoing impact of what police described as a potential "mass-casualty event" at a Perth rally.

items.title: Scientists hope bones in underwater 'time capsules' will unlock more prehistory
items.summary: Scientists who have been finding fossils in SA's underwater caves are hoping they can give a window into environmental changes.

items.title: Peregrine falcon carer prepares for avian influenza
items.summary: Peregrine falcons and wedge-tailed eagles could be among the first native species to contract a strain of bird flu that has recently arrived in Australia.

items.title: Laser technology developed to test alcoholic drinks for methanol
items.summary: Adelaide University researchers have developed a way to use lasers to test closed alcohol bottles for methanol, which kills hundreds of people every year.

items.title: Tasmania's electoral boundary shakeup leaves politicians deciding where to run
items.summary: Some notable politicians are among the 114,000 Tasmanians displaced by electoral boundary changes, and now have a difficult decision to make — stick with the electorate that voted them in, or move with the communities they've long been aligned with and live in.

items.title: Albanese welcomes AI companies to Australia on our terms. We just don't know what those are yet
items.summary: If Australia is truly going to coax major AI companies to choose us rather than the other suitor nations with similar ambitions, some tough decisions need to be made soon.

items.title: Businesses globally claw back $110 billion after Trump's tariffs deemed unlawful
items.summary: Businesses globally are clawing back tens of billions of dollars paid to United States customs after Donald Trump's so-called liberation day tariffs were deemed unlawful.

items.title: No need to panic about fuel prices as Iran war escalates again, analysts say
items.summary: With the price of oil rising and the fuel excise cut due to end, drivers are facing twin threats when it comes to the price of fuel, but experts say Australia is in a much better position compared with when the Middle East conflict began.

items.title: Telstra and telco regulator prepare for grilling over widespread outage
items.summary: Telstra prepares for a grilling in parliament about the recent network outage that disrupted Australia, with the communications regulator also set to face tough questions.

items.title: Liberals canvas ideas to address gender, youth and 'deep-rooted' multicultural problem
items.summary: Liberal Party HQ has admitted it is "in the political fight of our lives" as it canvasses ideas, including reviving gender quotas, to take the party forward.

items.title: AI office urged to include artists after Anthony Albanese's copyright promise
items.summary: The creative sector hopes there is a space to contribute to the Office of AI, given its content powers a lot of these tools.

items.title: Music quiz: Test your knowledge of instruments, musical terms and classical-inspired pop ballads
items.summary: From famous icons and musical terms to unusual instruments and classical influences in popular music, test your knowledge with this quiz.

items.title: I tried to use voodoo to win Race Around The World, and in some ways it worked
items.summary: Halfway through the Race Around The World competition, I'm second last on the leaderboard. So, I decide to cheat.

items.title: The Odyssey may be the epic we deserve, but not the one we need
items.summary: The scope of Christopher Nolan's latest epic might be awesome but a menagerie of supporting faces and questionable characterisations muddle the director's vision.

items.title: US launches new strikes at Iranian military targets in bid to secure Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: US Central Command says it launched a 90-minute attack targeting Iran's strategic Greater Tunb Island.

items.title: The future of AI
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Oil reserves eased Iran war price shock, but now they are 'spent', IMF says
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund says the global economy was spared the worst of the oil price shock because of large reserves. Now they are running out.

items.title: Boomanulla Oval in Narrabundah a step closer to Indigenous control
items.summary: The transition of ownership is part of a government agreement with the ACT Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Elected Body.

items.title: Medical records and personal details stolen in GP network cyber attack
items.summary: Partnered Health, which operates more than 50 general practice and skin cancer clinics across Australia, has identified more than a dozen clinics where it fears patient information was taken.

items.title: LGBTQIA+ pop-up club operating in former Sydney church ordered to 'cease offensive trading'
items.summary: The LGBTQIA+ venue says it is exploring its legal options after being issued with a breach of lease notice just days after opening at a former Sydney church.

items.title: Wine Australia's National Vintage Report 2026 finds lowest harvest since 2000
items.summary: Australian wine grape production has fallen to its lowest amount in more than 25 years as the industry adjusts to a combination of depressed market conditions.

items.title: Brisbane Broncos facing near-impossible task to reach NRL finals
items.summary: Brisbane must reach 13 wins to have any chance of playing finals in 2026, but even that wasn't enough for them in 2022 and may not be again this year.

items.title: New Zealand reports first case of H5N1 bird flu detected in brown skua seabird
items.summary: The deadly virus was detected in a brown skua seabird near Wellington, although authorities say there is not yet any evidence of mass mortality or impacts on poultry.

items.title: Trump reverses temporary pause on traffic stops after recent fatal shootings
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says federal immigration agents will continue carrying out vehicle stops, reversing a temporary pause announced by officials just a day earlier.

items.title: CDC races to track source of Cyclospora outbreak as cases spike in US
items.summary: The US CDC is investigating thousands of possible cases across 34 states, as officials track down the source of the outbreak.

items.title: US global trade investigation targets Australian lamb imports
items.summary: The United States International Trade Commission is investigating sheep meat imports, including from Australia, as American farmers struggle.

items.title: Donald Trump's actions in Iran this week show the world he's run out of options
items.summary: Donald Trump's actions this week have only confirmed he has run out of options and is now engaged in a simple war of wills, and calculations on who will run out of munitions first, with an enemy that he has underestimated from the start.

items.title: Daylight saving time could become permanent in the US. What's the situation in Australia?
items.summary: If the bill becomes law, it will eliminate the need for states to change the clock twice a year.

items.title: Labor aims to shape AI revolution with new data centre and AI copyright rules
items.summary: The prime minister unveils Labor's plan for new AI national standards, warning Australia has a narrow window to set AI's "social licence".

items.title: Perth plumber restrains alleged car thief in viral video, but lawyer warns of risk
items.summary: A Perth plumber has gone viral on social media after chasing down and restraining an alleged car thief after a botched getaway, but a prominent lawyer warns citizen's arrests are risky.

items.title: More than a quarter of Tasmanians will vote in new electorates, after AEC announces redistribution
items.summary: More than a quarter of Tasmanians will vote in new electorates under a long-awaited boundary redistribution announced by the Australian Electoral Commission.

items.title: Basil Zempilas declares any Labor loss in Secret Harbour by-election 'a win for Liberals'
items.summary: WA's Liberal leader says "there is no white flag being waved" in the upcoming Secret Harbour by-election, while indicating any result other than Labor retaining the seat, namely a win by One Nation, will be "a victory for the Liberals".

items.title: Disgraced ex-footballer Nicky Winmar removed from AFL Hall of Fame
items.summary: Winmar was found guilty earlier this month of three counts of unlawful assault and common law assault.

items.title: The sledge between Diamonds teammates that helped break the ice after Super Netball Grand Final
items.summary: Kate Moloney has had to put her team's Super Netball grand final nightmare aside to focus on the Commonwealth Games. Some gentle ribbing from her Aussie teammate has helped her see the lighter side.

items.title: Adelaide Parklands works to continue despite parliamentary inquiry
items.summary: Acting Premier Kyam Maher says works for the golf course redevelopment and MotoGP will continue despite a petition attracting 13,500 signatures.

items.title: The AFL is set on boutique stadiums for AFLW. Players think it is limiting the game
items.summary: The AFL has introduced four double-headers this season, meaning games will be played at bigger venues, while the league remains steadfast on boutique grounds for standalone matches.

items.title: Timor-Leste scam centre compounds uncovered as police arrest Chinese, Indonesians, Cambodians
items.summary: Police in Timor-Leste uncover a major international scam centre in the country, arresting more than 300 Chinese, Indonesian and Cambodian nationals in raids across the capital, Dili.

items.title: Mollie O'Callaghan reveals spinal stress fractures before Glasgow Commonwealth Games
items.summary: Mollie O'Callaghan reveals she has defied doctors' diagnoses to swim at the Commonwealth Games and Pan Pacific Championships despite spinal fractures and a shoulder injury.

items.title: Townsville gardener witnesses 'garbage guts' tree frog preying on sunbird
items.summary: A Townsville gardener was watering her garden when she witnessed a white-lipped tree frog catch a sunbird mid-flight.

items.title: Empty-nesters on how they 'fill the void' left by children
items.summary: Experts say there can be unexpected grief for parents that comes from children leaving home.

items.title: Kangaroo with joey shot with arrow at Lake Wallace
items.summary: Community members search for the injured animal, which was spooked off before wildlife volunteers could help.

items.title: US uses explosive one-way drone boats for first time in combat against Iran
items.summary: The United States is using one-way drone boats in combat against Iran for the first time in its military history. A defence expert says America is catching on to this "cheaper and more effective" tactic employed by Iran and Ukraine.

items.title: Flights bound for Adelaide cancelled and diverted due to thick fog
items.summary: A number of flights have been delayed for take-off at Adelaide Airport due to heavy fog while others were either cancelled or diverted elsewhere.

items.title: Adelaide Crows player Josh Worrell to face court over Port Road crash
items.summary: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell has been reported by police and will face court over a traffic crash last week.

items.title: New political parties are exploiting Victoria's archaic electoral system
items.summary: New political parties are seeking to exploit Victoria's anachronistic electoral system in the upper house to harvest voters' preferences and funnel them wherever they please.

items.title: Writer E Jean Carroll collects $8m in Trump sex abuse and defamation case
items.summary: Writer E Jean Carroll has collected nearly $US5.6 million ($8 million) from Donald Trump after a jury in 2023 found the US president liable for sexually abusing and defaming her, court records show.

items.title: Aerial cull prompts push from sporting shooters to target feral deer in WA national parks
items.summary: After a second annual aerial cull sparked controversy in southern WA, hunters say targeting feral deer from the ground is a more humane option.
